summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of the tour’s final night, with a monster first set highlighted by Cumberland Blues, Jack Straw, China > Rider and Playin’ in the Band, plus an epic Truckin’/The Other One suite.
review: This recording delivers an exceptionally clear, full‑spectrum soundboard mix that lets every nuance of the band shine. The show is widely praised as the tour’s pinnacle – a 19‑song first set packed with standout performances and a second‑set jam marathon featuring a smoking Truckin’/The Other One sequence and a soaring Morning Dew.

ai_rating.rationale: All six reviews give 5‑star ratings, consistently praising both the musical performance (monumental first set, epic jams) and the pristine soundboard source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as crystal clear, full‑spectrum with no patches; only a tiny cut at the very start of Not Fade Away and a brief tape flip on Morning Dew, but overall flawless.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A 19‑song first set that many call the finest of the tour, followed by a second‑set jam suite that flows seamlessly from Truckin' into an extended The Other One marathon and a powerful Morning Dew finale.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is described as soaring and perfectly locked with Keith; his lead work throughout the jams receives high praise.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass solo before The Other One intro is highlighted, and his counterpoint work is noted throughout the performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir provides strong rhythmic counterpoint and engages in memorable interplay with Garcia during the second set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux is called ‘at his absolute best’, sounding like Steve Winwood; Pigpen’s organ on Sing Me Back Home and the duet with Keith are singled out as exceptional.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drum solo is praised, and the overall percussion drive of the 35‑minute Other One segment is highlighted.
